title: Splitting only on first colon correctly handles ARN paths
description: opaque.find(':') splits on the first colon only, so paths containing colons (AWS ARNs like arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123:key/abc) are correctly captured as the path, not truncated. This is explicitly documented in the comment. A contributor might suggest splitting on '/' for path consistency — that would break ARN-style paths. Keep the first-colon split.
